name: UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship Round 9
eligibility: Must be at eligible UK research organisation. Transitioning to independence.
description: Supports early career researchers transitioning to research independence. 4-year fellowship.
notes: CRITICAL: Need university affiliation. Contact QUB/Ulster immediately. Stale status corrected by Aureon current sweep on 2026-07-09.

name: Innovate UK Smart Grant
eligibility: UK-registered businesses. No PhD required.
description: Supports disruptive innovations with commercial potential. Quarterly rounds.
notes: R&A Consulting can apply directly. Priority: Aureon AI commercialization.

name: National Space Innovation Programme
eligibility: UK organisations. SMEs encouraged.
description: Supports innovative space technology and services.
notes: Perfect fit for LSSP and EPAS. Contact innovation team.

name: AIRR Compute: AI Open Access
eligibility: UK registered businesses and eligible UK research organisations can apply for AIRR AI Open Access compute.
description: Compute-only access to AIRR resources including Isambard-AI for AI-driven research and scientific discovery.
notes: Portal draft created for proposal 0261-8797-7601-1. Final submit requires subsidy, peer-review, and company filing checks.

name: Wellcome Discovery Research
eligibility: Must be at eligible UK/EU research organisation.
description: Responsive-mode funding for discovery research.
notes: Need university affiliation. Strong fit for PEFCφS.

name: Leverhulme Trust Research Project Grants
eligibility: UK universities. Academic staff.
description: Supports original research in any discipline.
notes: Need university affiliation. Fundamental physics fit.

name: Horizon Europe EIC Accelerator
eligibility: EU-based companies. UK companies can participate (associate status).
description: Grants + equity for breakthrough innovations.
notes: Apply through Innovate UK as UK national contact point.

name: NATO DIANA Accelerator
eligibility: Dual-use technology companies.
description: Accelerator for deep tech with defence applications.
notes: 2027 cohort. Space Solar Power is NATO priority. Prepare now.

name: NASA SBIR Phase I
eligibility: US companies or US subsidiaries. Can partner with US firm.
description: Small Business Innovation Research for space technology.
notes: Partner with US space company needed. Space Solar has connections.

name: Templeton Foundation Science & Big Questions
eligibility: Open. International researchers welcome.
description: Supports research at the intersection of science and big questions (consciousness, meaning, etc.).
notes: PERFECT FIT. Your HNC framework and consciousness research are exactly what they fund.

name: Schmidt Sciences / Schmidt Futures
eligibility: Open. Interdisciplinary research.
description: Supports ambitious interdisciplinary research.
notes: Your interdisciplinary AI + physics + neuroscience is exactly what they want.

name: Simons Foundation Collaboration Grants
eligibility: Open. Mathematics and physical sciences focus.
description: Supports mathematics, physics, and life sciences.
notes: HNC harmonic mathematics and physics are strong fit.

name: ARPA-E Energy Research
eligibility: US entities. International collaboration.
description: Supports high-risk energy technology research.
notes: LSSP space energy and Illumination Chip. PERFECT FIT.

name: ESA Open Space Innovation Platform
eligibility: ESA member states. UK is associate member.
description: Supports innovative space technology ideas.
notes: LSSP and EPAS for ESA missions. UK associate member.

name: ESA Space Solutions - Business Applications
eligibility: ESA member states. SMEs encouraged.
description: Supports commercial applications of space technology.
notes: LSSP commercial space energy. R&A Consulting as SME.

name: NIH BRAIN Initiative
eligibility: US institutions. International collaboration.
description: Supports neuroscience and brain research.
notes: PEFCφS neural coherence and consciousness.

name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative Science Grants
eligibility: Open. International researchers welcome.
description: Supports open science, neuroscience, and biomedical research.
notes: Your open-science philosophy and PEFCφS research are strong fit.

name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
eligibility: Open. AI for good focus.
description: Supports AI for social good and neuroscience.
notes: Aureon AI + PEFCφS neuroscience = strong fit.

name: Next Wave: Breakthrough Wave 1
eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants or collaborations. To lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprise (SME) claiming grant funding on this application. To work alone your organisation must be a UK registered SME .
description: UK registered businesses can apply for a share of up to £10 million for industrial research projects to explore industrial strategy aligned Createch innovation. This funding is from Innovate UK.
notes: Live application 10209601 was submitted through the Innovation Funding Service. Do not reopen unless a deliberate resubmission decision is made. | IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Already submitted for the Aureon Evidence Studio route; monitor assessment and do not duplicate. Next action: Monitor submitted IFS application 10209601. name: UK-Switzerland CR&D Round 3
eligibility: This competition is open to UK and Swiss collaborations only. To lead a project your organisation must be a UK registered: business of any size must be or involve at least one grant claiming UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprise (SME) collaborate with a Swiss implementation partner and at least one research institute
description: UK registered businesses can apply for a share of up to £3 million for innovative projects in specific technology areas. You must collaborate with at least one Swiss implementation partner applying under the equivalent Swiss Innosuisse programme.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Potential collaborative route; cannot be led alone because Swiss implementation and research partners are required. name: Scaling performance of quantum computing hardware: CR&D
eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only. To lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered business of any size or a research and technology organisation (RTO).
description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £33 million to develop device level hardware and associated software to meet the scale, programmability, and runtime performance needs of universal fault tolerant quantum computers.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Indirect fit through software/runtime assurance only; needs quantum hardware lead partner. Next action: Prepare partner approach note; do not submit standalone. Raw closes text: 18 September 2026. name: DRIVE35 Innovation Fund: Collaborate 3
eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only.
description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £20 million for late stage collaborative research and development projects. Projects will support the UK’s transition to zero emission vehicles and a pathway to a net zero automotive industry.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Possible only with transport/manufacturing partner. Current Aureon evidence is enabling software, not vehicle hardware. Next action: Log partner-required route; no standalone submission. Raw closes text: 16 September 2026. name: CfI: Earlier identification of UK children with SEN
eligibility: Organisations of any size can apply as a single legal entity and may use specialist subcontractors. Project costs must be GBP200,000 to GBP500,000 inclusive of VAT.
description: Procurement R&D contract to develop innovations enabling earlier, fairer and more reliable identification of SEN.
notes: Standalone application route is possible. Do not claim confirmed education partners. Proposal uses funded co-design and specialist safeguarding/SEN review.

name: Future Leaders Fellowships: Round 11, business and non-academic
eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants with supporting organisations. To host a Future Leaders Fellowship your UK-registered organisation must be a: business of any size charity catapult public body agri-tech centre eligible research organisation
description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £110 million to deliver ambitious research and innovation and develop future leaders. This funding is from UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: High-value next pack. Business/non-academic route can frame Gary/Aureon as a future leader if host/organisational eligibility is satisfied. name: DRIVE35: Scale Up Fund
eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants and collaborations. To lead a project your organisation must be a UK registered business of any size. Non-UK registered businesses may apply but must set up a UK registered business before any grant can be awarded. The UK registered business must deliver your project. You will not be able to start your project until the business is set up with UK staff in place.
description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £150 million, to create scale up manufacturing facilities for zero emission vehicle technologies.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Possible only with transport/manufacturing partner. Current Aureon evidence is enabling software, not vehicle hardware. Next action: Log partner-required route; no standalone submission. Raw closes text: No submission deadline.

name: Consumer Led Flexibility for the Clean Energy Superpower Mission
eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants and collaborations. description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £25 million for pre-commercialisation projects. This funding is from the UKRI R&D Missions Accelerator Programme through the Clean Energy Superpower Mission.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Potential fit if Aureon is scoped as consumer-energy flexibility evidence, assurance, and orchestration tooling. name: Innovation Loans Expression of Interest
eligibility: This competition is open to single applications from UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprises (SMEs) only.
description: UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprises can submit an Expression of Interest for loans for close to market innovative projects with strong commercial potential. They must significantly improve the UK economy and drive societal benefit.
notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Useful non-grant finance route, but loan risk requires a finance-readiness check before submission. Next action: Prepare EOI narrative; flag as loan, not grant. Raw closes text: No submission deadline.

eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only.

name: Accelerated Knowledge Transfer Partnerships 6 (AKT 6)
eligibility: To lead a project your organisation must: be a UK registered higher education or further education institution, research and technology organisation (RTO) or Catapult collaborate with a UK registered business of any size with four or more full time employees The business must contribute a minimum of 10% to the project’s costs.

name: Commercialising Knowledge Assets Fund (CKAF) Autumn
eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants. Grants shall be awarded to, and administered by, the applicant organisation.
